title: We should believe in Reincarnation to accept who we are and celebrate diversity

transcript.text: Hello. I think I made this video before, but I want to make it again.
One reason why you should not be an atheist, and you should not be...
Maybe I can put it in the same video. A Christian who thinks that you live on Earth...
Oh wait, no. I'll do the atheist. You shouldn't be an atheist.
Now, for the purposes of this video, an atheist is somebody who thinks you live once,
And then you're never going to live again.
One reason why you should believe in reincarnation is that you can enjoy other people's successes.
You can enjoy other people's lives, even though you're not going to live their life if you believe in reincarnation.
Because if you believe in reincarnation, you know that in another life, you will live their life.
And you will do their thing. So there's no worries.
Because it's impossible for a human being to live every different archetypal lifestyle.
You can't be a badass athlete in all of the sports, a movie star, a politician, a world traveler, a parent, all in one life.
You can't. We don't live long enough for that.
But the idea is that we should enjoy all of those aspects, all of those archetypal lifestyles and personalities.
And I only named a few.
We should enjoy all of those.
But you can't thoroughly enjoy it if you don't feel like you can experience it, or you will experience it, or you have experienced it yourself.
You can live somebody else's life vicariously and celebrate.
You can be like me and just be single, homeless, and at the same time be just as happy as your friend who has a job and children.
And you can celebrate them and you can live the life of having a job and children because your friend has it and look at it and be happy and feel like you have the job and children.
And vice versa.
They can feel like they have your life.
And you can mutually respect and enjoy each other's lives that way.
More if you believe in reincarnation because you believe that – because it's like, oh, yeah, isn't that cool that you're doing that?
Yeah, I'll get there one day.
I'll do that too.
But if you think that you won't do that ever, then you'll feel like your life isn't complete.
And somebody might say, oh, but, you know, your life is your life and you're this and that, you're that.
But then that – but if one person is something, one archetype, and the other guy is that other thing and it's only once and forever, that separates you.
That divides you.
That makes things different, people different forever, which causes – subconsciously causes division and strife.
And then people start comparing quality of life and to each other and it's not healthy.
It divides you.
So if you believe in reincarnation and you believe that you will be all of those things once in your life, in the eternity of time,
And every – but not just you, but everybody else will be all of the archetypes and live all of the different jobs and activities and everything,
Then that makes – that brings everybody together.
It makes everybody the same.
And I don't mean the same as in the Borg where they all think the same and they all –
Because with the Borg, they're not individuals.
They're all like following the group mind.
But if you believe that you can do everything and you're all the same
And you have reincarnation and the eternal time to live out all of those fantasies,
Then you're still – you're an individual and you can – you control your own destiny and your own activities in life.
So it's empowering.
So it's empowering.
It's uniting.
And it makes you realize that you're the same as everybody else and that you're actually all part of the same –
You're all different parts of the same being that's alive, that's the universe, that I call God.
And I think most – I think everybody who says God in some form of their mind, that's what they're thinking.
Who believes in God, that's what they're thinking.
And some people who say God, like even in the conscious part of their mind, that's what they're thinking.
So that's one important reason why you should believe in reincarnation
And why you should not be an atheist because atheists believe that you live once and then you're gone.
And they're so happy thinking that and it's like their MO.
It's like their modus operandum.
It's like their goal in life.
And I do not understand.
It's something that I lay in bed at night thinking, trying to comprehend how somebody can be happy
With being an atheist.
Somebody can be happy thinking that when they die, they're gone forever and their soul is gone forever.
And that makes looking at death just scary.
It makes death death.
I mean, we're not – we're not – it diminishes you.
If all you are is your body, I mean, you live for 70 years and then you're gone?
That's such – that's so puny.
That's such puny thought.
And it's so – it's so contradicting to reality.
Get a telescope and look out there.
The world is not small.
The universe is very large.
I don't even have to explain it.
You know how large the universe is.
We all know the whole analogy of sand on the earth to how many stars there are.
There's built – there's an infinite number of stars, therefore, an infinite number of worlds.
And if everything in this world communicates, then everything out there communicates.
And it's not one.
And it's not separate.
It's one.
And as far as, you know, the whole idea of you live once and then you go to heaven or you reincarnate in heaven but you don't reincarnate on earth,
That is limited thinking as well because everything happens gradually.
Everything on this thing happens gradually.
It takes you 15 years to grow.
You can't grow – you don't go to sleep one night at three feet tall and wake up at six feet tall the next morning.
So why would you do that in the larger scheme of the world?
When you imagine heaven, why not just bring heaven to the earth instead of imagine yourself dying and then all of a sudden you're in heaven and you get whatever you want?
Why not just bring heaven down here?
Why not just – you imagine heaven.
Oh, what's heaven to a Christian?
Fundamentalist Christian.
What's heaven, do you ask them?
Heaven is where you snap your fingers and you have whatever you want and you live forever and, you know, no more sickness.
Well, look around.
We can have that.

If you do the math and you figure out – tally out the scores, if we don't spend money on wars and on security and on all these defensive things because people are constantly going after each other and we figure out free energy, which I think exists and all of that,
Then we will live in a world that's exactly how a fundamentalist Christian would describe heaven.
So this world is heaven.
It just gradually moves in to the heaven where a fundamentalist Christian would describe.
It's gradual.
So when you die and you're reborn, you can reborn in this world that is similar to the heaven that you're talking about.
So what does that tell you?
It tells you fundamentalist Christians believe in reincarnation.
They just don't know it.
Okay.
Oops.
That's it.
So yes, believe in reincarnation.
Don't be an atheist and don't be a simplistic fundamentalist Christian.
Who thinks heaven is so much different from the earth?
Okay.
Okay.
